title: Vegan Sensitive Skincare
description: Which vegan skincare products are best for sensitive skin while also being eco‑friendly?
narrative: User seeks product recommendations that meet three intertwined criteria: botanical, cruelty‑free formulations suitable for sensitive skin, and sustainable packaging. Relevance is determined by ingredients listed as vegan, dermatologist‑approved for sensitivity, and evidence of eco‑certifications such as B Corp, Fair Trade, or recyclable/biodegradable packaging. Documents that offer generic lists, non‑vegan items, or lack clarity on skin‑sensitivity should be considered irrelevant.

title: Sustainable Agriculture Policies
description: What are the most recent international policies addressing sustainable agriculture practices?
narrative: The user wants up-to‑date information on global policy initiatives that promote sustainable agriculture, focusing on legislation, regulatory frameworks, and incentive programs from the last five years. Relevant documents should discuss specific policy measures, their implementation status, and impacts on agricultural sustainability.

title: Electric Vehicle Subsidies
description: How can manufacturers and consumers access subsidies for electric vehicles?
narrative: The user seeks a comprehensive overview of subsidy programs available to both EV producers and owners, including eligibility criteria, application procedures, financial benefits, and real‑world examples. Relevant sources should discuss specific subsidy schemes, their scopes, and influence on EV adoption; materials that merely treat EVs broadly or focus on unrelated aspects are considered irrelevant.

title: Explainable AI Healthcare
description: How can explainable AI techniques be applied to improve clinical decision support systems in healthcare?
narrative: The user seeks a comprehensive understanding of how explainable AI (XAI) methods—such as SHAP, LIME, counterfactual explanations, and rule‑based models—can be integrated into clinical decision support systems to enhance interpretability, trust, and compliance with healthcare regulations. Relevance criteria include peer‑reviewed papers or conference proceedings that (1) present empirical studies or case reports applying XAI to medical data (e.g., electronic health records, imaging, genomics), (2) describe evaluation procedures for interpretability (e.g., fidelity, human‑evaluation studies, regulatory alignment), and (3) discuss challenges like bias mitigation, data privacy, and workflow integration. Works that only provide high‑level overviews, non‑empirical commentary, or focus solely on industrial implementations without methodological detail are considered irrelevant.

title: Car Battery Aging
description: Which indicators can reliably predict a car battery’s imminent failure before a full shutdown occurs?
narrative: The user is looking for practical, evidence‑based criteria to spot early degradation in automotive lead‑acid batteries. They want measurable diagnostics—such as voltage curves, temperature and electrolyte level changes, alternator performance and drive‑cycle patterns—and threshold values that signal impending failure. Relevance requires detailed technical explanations, real‑world case studies, and manufacturer guidelines, while excluding purely anecdotal or opinion‑based content.

title: Eco-Design Architecture
description: What are the best practices for designing sustainable buildings that minimize environmental impact while ensuring occupant comfort?
narrative: The user seeks detailed guidance on eco-friendly architectural design, including strategies for energy efficiency, material selection, building envelope performance, water conservation, and integration of renewable technologies. Relevance should be assessed by sources that provide up-to-date design guidelines, case studies, and evidence of environmental performance (e.g., LEED, BREEAM, Green Star) rather than purely theoretical or unrelated architectural content.

title: Blockchain Regulation
description: What legal frameworks govern cryptocurrency exchanges in the EU?
narrative: The user seeks detailed information on current EU directives, national regulations, and relevant case law affecting crypto exchanges. Relevance requires documents that discuss regulatory bodies, enforcement actions, and compliance guidelines, and excludes general opinion pieces or technical product reviews.

title: Enrollment Form Fields
description: Which data fields are typically included in online student enrollment forms and what privacy policies accompany such data collection?
narrative: I am preparing documentation for a new university enrollment portal and need a comprehensive list of standard form fields—such as student first name, age, country of residence, educational attainment, and interests—as well as the associated privacy statements that govern the processing of participants’ personal data under GDPR. Relevant sources will enumerate these fields verbatim and provide the wording of privacy notices; unrelated content that merely lists other topics or unrelated student data will be excluded.

title: Farm Climate Grants
description: What government schemes provide financial assistance to farmers for implementing climate‑resilient practices?
narrative: The user is a smallholder farmer seeking up‑to‑date information on specific subsidy programs, including eligibility criteria, funding amounts, application deadlines, required documentation, and recent success stories. Relevant documents should contain detailed policy descriptions, eligibility tables, application guidelines, and case studies from the past five years. Non‑relevant material such as general climate reports, unrelated agricultural manuals, or purely academic research should be excluded.

title: Multi-Currency Management
description: Can I use AXA's online banking to hold and transfer multiple foreign currency accounts, and what fees or limits apply to these services?
narrative: The user needs detailed information on the capabilities and restrictions of managing foreign currency in AXA's client space, including fee structures, supported currencies, transfer limits, and necessary steps to set up such accounts. Relevance should be judged by presence of these specific operational details and any comparisons with domestic services.

title: Arctic Marine Impact
description: How does climate change affect species diversity and distribution in the Arctic marine ecosystem?
narrative: User seeks scientific reports and peer-reviewed articles that analyze temperature rise, ice melt, and salinity changes affecting Arctic marine biodiversity, focusing on specific species shifts, ecosystem productivity, and potential conservation strategies.

title: Semi-Early Potatoes
description: Which semi‑early potato varieties are optimal for greenhouse cultivation with high yields and disease resistance?
narrative: The user seeks detailed, up‑to‑date information on semi‑early potato cultivars that perform well in greenhouse environments, offering robust yield, resistance to common diseases such as downy mildew, and versatility for various cooking methods. Relevant content must include growing duration, yield data, disease resistance ratings, and practical greenhouse cultivation guidance.

title: EU Synthetic Biology Regulation
description: Where can I find comparative analyses of synthetic biology regulations across European Union member states?
narrative: The user is looking for authoritative sources that compare how each EU country approaches the regulation of synthetic biology. Relevance criteria include documents that (1) discuss legal frameworks or regulatory guidelines for synthetic biology within multiple EU member states, (2) present a side‑by‑side comparison or synthesis of key differences and similarities, (3) include recent updates or legislative proposals, and (4) provide expert commentary or case studies illustrating how these regulations are applied in practice. This requires scholarly or policy‑focused materials rather than general overviews or unrelated content.

title: AI Labor Disruption
description: What are the projected effects of artificial intelligence on employment sectors in the next decade?
narrative: The user wants a comprehensive overview of AI-driven automation's impact on various industries, including quantitative forecasts, policy responses, and real-world case studies. Relevance criteria: recent empirical research, credible industry reports, academic analyses, and policy documents that discuss socio-economic implications of machine learning and robotics on the workforce.

title: Alpine Lichen Plastics
description: How does microplastic contamination affect the physiology and distribution of alpine lichen species in the European Alps?
narrative: The user is a conservation biologist seeking up‑to‑date, peer‑reviewed evidence on microplastic uptake in lichens. Relevant sources must be primary research (field surveys, controlled lab experiments, or meta‑analyses) published within the last five years, focusing on European alpine regions, and reporting measurable endpoints such as growth rates, photosynthetic efficiency, or contaminant bioaccumulation. Non‑scientific reports, opinion pieces, or studies outside the Alpine context are considered irrelevant.

title: Eco-Friendly Housing Trends
description: What are the recent trends and key factors driving eco-friendly housing adoption in urban areas?
narrative: The user wants up-to-date information on eco‑housing market trends, including technological innovations, economic incentives, policy frameworks, and consumer attitudes, and seeks data that includes studies, reports, and case examples published within the last five years.

title: Remote Tribe Media Ethics
description: What are the ethical considerations and best practices for representing remote indigenous communities in travel documentaries?
narrative: The user aims to locate academic studies, policy papers, and industry guidelines that discuss cultural sensitivity, informed consent, potential cultural disruption, and portrayal accuracy in media productions focused on nomadic or isolated peoples, plus insight into how such representations affect the communities involved.

title: Microplastics in Oceans
description: What are the primary ecological impacts of microplastic pollution on marine ecosystems, and how do different particle sizes influence species health?
narrative: The user seeks detailed, evidence‑based studies on microplastics’ effects on marine biodiversity, covering trophic transfer, reproductive health, and bioaccumulation across diverse taxa. Relevant documents must provide empirical data, controlled experiments, meta‑analyses, or comprehensive reviews that examine various microplastic sizes and types. Sources limited to policy summaries or anecdotal reports without scientific backing are considered less relevant.

title: Rural Fiber Investment
description: What are the economic and policy considerations for rural municipalities in France when investing in fiber‑to‑the‑home (FTTH) infrastructure?
narrative: A local government officer seeks detailed analysis on the trade‑offs of investing in fiber networks for rural towns, including cost, return on investment, available government funding, alignment with national broadband plans, and best‑practice case studies that inform strategic decision‑making.

title: Solar Incentive Comparison
description: Which states offer the best financial incentives for installing residential solar panels, and what are the specific eligibility requirements?
narrative: The user wants an up‑to‑date, side‑by‑side overview of state‑level solar incentives—including tax credits, rebates, net‑metering rules, and enrollment procedures—to determine the most advantageous options for a potential residential installation. Documents must provide details on incentive types, eligibility thresholds, application steps, and time‑frames.

title: EV Leasing Incentives
description: I want to understand how French government bonuses, trade‑in allowances, and leasing terms affect the total cost of a 25‑month LLD on a Kia e‑Niro.
narrative: Relevance: the answer should break down the bonus calculations, lease payment schedule, trade‑in scenarios, mileage limits, and recent policy changes. The user expects explicit cost figures and clear assumptions needed to replicate the analysis.

title: Privacy in Smart Homes
description: What privacy risks and data handling practices exist for smart home devices, and how can users protect themselves?
narrative: The user is looking for detailed information on how smart home devices collect, store, and share personal data, including technical mechanisms such as encryption and cloud storage, industry privacy standards, regulatory requirements (e.g., GDPR, CCPA), and real-world case studies of data breaches. They want sources that analyze privacy risks, recommend best practices for users, and discuss how manufacturers and service providers can improve transparency and user control.

title: Urban Rooftop Farming
description: How can urban rooftop farms improve city food security and reduce carbon footprints?
narrative: The user seeks evidence-based insights on the design, feasibility, and impact of rooftop agricultural projects in metropolitan areas, including case studies, cost-benefit analyses, policy incentives, and barriers to adoption.

title: Car Sharing vs Ownership Sustainability
description: How do the environmental impacts of car sharing compare to those of private car ownership in urban settings?
narrative: The user intends to compare CO2 emissions, fuel efficiency, and resource consumption between car sharing fleets and privately owned vehicles, considering usage frequency, vehicle mix, and infrastructure needs, to evaluate which model offers a lower ecological footprint.

title: Urban Microclimate Modeling
description: What are the most effective techniques for modeling urban microclimates in rapidly expanding megacities?
narrative: The user is a city planner seeking advanced computational approaches to simulate streets‑scale temperature, airflow, and pollution dispersion under different zoning scenarios. Relevant documents should discuss high‑resolution CFD, LIDAR data integration, case studies from at least two global cities, and guidelines for translating simulation results into policy decisions.

title: Recovery Drinks for Runners
description: What natural, low-sugar beverages can support muscle recovery after a marathon?
narrative: The user is a marathon runner looking for drinks that aid recovery without added sugar or preservatives. They prefer natural ingredients and want to know which products are suitable for post-competition hydration and muscle repair. Answers should name specific drinks, list ingredients, provide evidence or sources, and assess suitability for post-run recovery.

title: Sustainable Fashion Trends
description: What are the most effective sustainable clothing practices adopted by major fashion brands in 2024?
narrative: The user wants up‑to‑date, evidence‑based information on sustainable manufacturing, material sourcing, supply‑chain transparency, and consumer impact. Relevant documents should discuss specific initiatives, certifications, ethical considerations, and measurable outcomes, and provide comparisons across brands and geographic regions.

title: Warranty for E‑Tech Electric
description: What are the parts and labor coverage terms, battery coverage, and any extensions available for a used 2022 Renault Mégane E‑Tech Electric under the 12‑month, 24‑month, and 36‑month warranty options?
narrative: The user is a prospective buyer looking to evaluate the long‑term protection of a used 2022 Renault Mégane E‑Tech Electric. They need a comparative overview of the base 12‑month warranty and the optional 24‑month and 36‑month extensions, focusing on what is covered (parts, labor, battery), the duration, any limits or exclusions, and how battery degradation is addressed. Relevance requires explicit coverage details, duration, battery terms, and side‑by‑side comparison that explains how the different packages impact the overall risk of ownership. Items that simply list warranty options without detailed comparison are insufficient.

title: Multilingual Privacy Notices
description: Which scholarly articles evaluate the comprehension and effectiveness of multilingual privacy notices for users with varying literacy levels?
narrative: A policy designer seeks academic evidence to guide the creation of user‑friendly privacy notices that are available in multiple languages. Relevant documents should be peer‑reviewed studies published since 2005 that focus specifically on multilingual privacy notices for web or app interfaces, include empirical data on user comprehension, recall, or perceived trust, and assess the impact of language choice on user understanding and decision‑making. Non‑empirical commentaries, news articles, or case studies without measurable outcomes are considered irrelevant.

title: Climate Agricultural Impact
description: What are the projected impacts of climate change on wheat yields in North America over the next 30 years?
narrative: The user seeks scholarly analyses, scenario modeling, and region‑specific studies that forecast wheat yield changes under various climate projections. Relevance is judged by the presence of peer‑reviewed articles, conference proceedings, or reputable reports detailing quantitative yield forecasts, adaptation strategies, and policy implications for North American wheat production.

title: Car Rental Profitability
description: What key factors determine the profitability of renting a personal car on peer‑to‑peer platforms such as Getaround?
narrative: The user seeks an in‑depth analysis of profitability influences, including expected rental income, seasonal demand variations, platform fees, insurance and maintenance costs, vehicle suitability (model, mileage, age), and the impact of features like Getaround Connect. Relevance requires the discussion to cover financial metrics, cost structures, risk mitigation, and practical tips for maximizing return on a personal vehicle. Documents should provide quantitative data, comparisons, and actionable guidance for owners considering or already renting their cars.

title: ML Privacy Techniques
description: What practical methods can be used to protect sensitive data within a machine‑learning pipeline?
narrative: The user seeks a concise overview of current privacy‑preserving techniques applicable to large‑scale ML workflows. Relevant documents should cover differential privacy, federated learning, secure multi‑party computation, and real‑world deployment case studies, including implementation guidance and evaluation metrics.

title: Visual‑Access Windows
description: Which Bordeaux National Opera concerts in December 2021 were marked as accessible for visual impairments and scheduled on Saturday or Sunday?
narrative: The search is constrained to the December 2021 calendar of the Bordeaux National Opera. Only events that explicitly list "Visual disability" in their accessibility metadata should be considered. Weekend dates (Saturday and Sunday) are required, and cancelled or non‑accessible listings must be excluded. The user seeks a concise set of event titles, dates, and accessibility confirmation to plan attendance or recommend inclusive cultural options.

title: Volunteer Grants in French Football
description: Which programs provide financial support for volunteers in the Indre‑et‑Loire football district, and what are the eligibility requirements and application procedures?
narrative: The user is a volunteer coordinator seeking information on available grant opportunities within the French football league system. They need to know specific programs run by the district or federations, the amount of funding, service thresholds, required documentation, deadlines, and contact details. Relevance requires documents that describe grant schemes, eligibility criteria, application timelines, and any recent updates due to COVID‑19.

title: Energy Cheque Eligibility
description: Which households are eligible for the French energy cheque and what income thresholds apply?
narrative: The user is searching for detailed eligibility criteria for France’s energy cheque program, including permissible household income ranges, household size requirements, and how these parameters are applied in the allocation process. Relevance is judged by documents that precisely outline the thresholds, the calculation method for household income, and any changes to the program over recent fiscal years.

title: Video Conferencing Accessibility
description: How can video conferencing platforms meet emerging accessibility standards for users with auditory and visual impairments?
narrative: The user is an accessibility specialist preparing a compliance roadmap for a SaaS company. They require details on current WCAG 2.1 and EN 301 549 clauses applicable to video conferencing, specific UI enhancements, live captioning support, screen‑reader interaction, and performance benchmarks. Relevance demands up‑to‑date guidelines, vendor case studies, technical implementation notes, and evidence of effectiveness.

title: Climate Risk Communication
description: What best practices exist for communicating climate change risks to policymakers and the public?
narrative: The user needs evidence‑based strategies for climate risk communication, preferring peer‑reviewed literature, white papers, and recent case studies (within the last decade). Non‑technical media reports, generic PR materials, or content older than ten years are deemed less relevant.

title: Leek Pie Alternatives
description: How can a standard leek pie be adapted for vegetarian or vegan diets, including suitable ingredient substitutions and cooking adjustments?
narrative: The user seeks guidance on transforming a traditional leek pie recipe to meet dietary restrictions, wants specific ingredient swap recommendations, cooking time adjustments, and flavor preservation details, with relevance focusing on substitutions that maintain texture and taste.

title: Urban green roofs
description: How do green roofs contribute to sustainable city development, and what are the best practices for their implementation?
narrative: The user is researching the role of green roofs in urban sustainability, interested in environmental benefits, economic feasibility, maintenance considerations, and policy incentives. Relevance requires documents that discuss ecological impact, cost‐benefit analysis, retrofit guidelines, regulatory frameworks, and case studies of successful green roof projects.

title: Arce Eligibility Criteria
description: What documents and conditions are required to qualify for the Arce funding when starting a business?
narrative: The user is looking for a comprehensive, step‑by‑step guide to the eligibility requirements for the Arce programme, including the necessary forms, proof of business creation, employment status, and how existing unemployment benefits interact with the capital award. Relevant documents must detail the calculation of the capital amount, the 45% rule, the 3% deduction, and the timing of payments, as well as any restrictions or exclusions that could affect the applicant.

title: EV Charging Regulation
description: What are the current EU policies on electric vehicle charging infrastructure and how do they affect small businesses?
narrative: The user is a small business owner planning to install EV charging stations and wants detailed information on EU regulations, incentives, compliance requirements, and potential financial support.

title: Lamp Fuel Comparison
description: Which fuel is safest, least smoky, and most odor‑free for home oil lamps?
narrative: The user wants a comprehensive comparison of common oil lamp fuels – such as kerosene, paraffin, mineral oil, vegetable oils, and scented oils – based on criteria like safety (flammability and toxicity), smoke production, odor, soot accumulation, cost, and availability. Relevant answers should provide empirical data, user or expert reviews, safety guidelines, and practical recommendations for selecting a suitable fuel for household lamp use.

title: VR Rehab Evidence
description: What is the clinical evidence for the effectiveness of virtual reality interventions in physical rehabilitation of stroke patients?
narrative: The user seeks to compare the efficacy of virtual reality (VR) based rehabilitation programs with conventional therapy for adult stroke survivors. Relevance criteria include peer‑reviewed studies (RCTs and systematic reviews) published within the last 15 years that report standardized outcome measures such as the Fugl‑Meyer Assessment, Functional Ambulation Category, or Upper Extremity Fugl‑Meyer score. Studies should involve adult participants (18+) with ischemic or hemorrhagic stroke and provide quantitative effect sizes, confidence intervals, and statistical significance. Exclusion criteria are studies involving pediatric or traumatic brain injury populations, non‑clinical VR use, or non‑English language publications. The goal is to synthesize high‑quality evidence to inform clinical decision‑making or policy development regarding VR integration into stroke rehabilitation protocols.

title: Remote Mental Health
description: How effective are remote mental health services in delivering outcomes comparable to in-person therapy for adults with moderate anxiety?
narrative: The user is looking for research evidence comparing the clinical effectiveness, patient adherence, and cost-effectiveness of teletherapy versus traditional face‑to‑face counseling among adults diagnosed with moderate anxiety disorders. Relevant documents should present empirical studies, meta‑analyses, or systematic reviews that report measurable outcomes such as symptom reduction, dropout rates, and economic metrics, and should discuss contextual factors such as technology barriers, demographics, and practice settings.

title: Migration Health Impact
description: What are the health impacts of recent migration flows on host countries, including disease prevalence, access to care, and health system strain?
narrative: The user seeks comparative data and analyses that quantify health outcomes for migrant populations versus native residents in host countries. Documents are relevant if they present measurable health metrics (e.g., morbidity, mortality, service utilization), are derived from credible studies or official reports, and are stratified by migrant status and country context, published within the last decade.
